D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Contrast Tension Score

turn three of Itten's contrasts into one number — and watch which axis is carrying the tension between two colours

Three of Itten's contrasts can be read straight off a colour pair: complementary (hue opposition), light-dark (value gap), and saturation. Combine them into a single tension score and the stacked bar shows which axis dominates. Warm-cool and extension are deliberately left out — they are not properties of a bare colour pair.

Set the hue and lightness of each colour. The headline is the combined tension; the bar splits it into hue, value, and saturation terms. It operationalises Itten's principles — a design heuristic, not a measured psychophysical quantity.
